summary: First iteration. Tested LifeCue AI backend (23 pytest cases) and frontend E2E flow (signup → onboarding → today → coach → subscription → profile → logout). 22/23 backend tests pass. Frontend basics work end-to-end EXCEPT AI Coach which is broken due to invalid model name in TIER_MODELS. Subscription page upgrade flow works at the API layer but the UI lacks data-testid on plan selection buttons (could not be triggered via testid). Onboarding habit chips and Today reminder/habit-tile buttons also lack testids. React duplicate-key warning observed on Today (likely water/habit list rendering).

critical.issue: AI Coach fails with 500: 'Invalid model name passed in model=gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite'. The model id is wrong / unsupported by the Emergent LLM gateway. Likely needs to be `gemini-2.0-flash` (or `gemini-2.0-flash-lite`). Affects all 3 tiers if same pattern; quick tier confirmed broken.

minor.issue: Razorpay SDK import fails at runtime: 'No module named pkg_resources' (setuptools missing). Currently masked by mock-fallback, but if a real RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET is configured later, real order creation will still fail. Add `setuptools` to requirements or use razorpay HTTP API directly.
Pydantic EmailStr rejects `.test` reserved TLDs — fine in production, but worth documenting.

ui_bugs.issue: Sending message renders 'I couldn't reply this time. Coach unavailable: ...Invalid model name...' — propagates raw backend 500. Root cause is backend invalid model id (see backend critical).
React warning: 'Encountered two children with the same key' (4x). Likely duplicate `key` in water_logs entries map or reminders list — fix React keys.
Plan selection buttons (Monthly/Yearly upgrade CTAs) have NO data-testid (only plans-title, credits-pill, plan-faq exist). Cannot drive upgrade flow via testid in E2E.
Individual habit chips on the habits step lack data-testid (no `onboard-habit-*`). Visually clickable but not testable.
Habit tiles and reminder Done/Snooze/Skip buttons lack individual data-testids (e.g., `habit-tile-water`, `reminder-done-*`). Buttons work via DOM order but aren't reliably targetable.

integration_issues.issue: Real LLM call fails for quick tier with Invalid model. Daily/Deep tiers very likely fail too (gpt-5-mini/claude-sonnet-4-5 model ids should be re-verified against Emergent gateway).

issues: Reminder action buttons (check / snooze-snowflake / X) are icon-only with no aria-label/tooltip; could hurt a11y.
Looks clean; hero contrast OK; no major issues.

action_items: FIX backend TIER_MODELS in /app/backend/server.py: replace `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` with a model id supported by the Emergent LLM gateway (e.g., `gemini-2.0-flash` or `gemini-2.0-flash-lite`). Verify `gpt-5-mini` and `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` are also valid for daily/deep tiers — fall back to known-good ids if not.

ADD data-testid attributes: `plan-monthly-btn`, `plan-yearly-btn` (Subscription.jsx); `onboard-habit-{key}` (Onboarding habits step); `habit-tile-{key}` (Today habits grid); `reminder-done-{id}`, `reminder-snooze-{id}`, `reminder-skip-{id}` (Today reminders).
FIX duplicate React `key` warning on Today (check water entries and reminders/habits rendering — use stable unique ids).
ADD `setuptools` to backend requirements.txt so razorpay SDK import works when real secret is configured.
FRIENDLY ERROR: When Coach call fails, show a generic friendly message instead of leaking backend exception text in `coach-messages`.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 645 lines — within limit but approaching threshold; consider splitting into routers (auth, habits, water, coach, billing).
CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] together with allow_credentials=True — browsers ignore wildcard with credentials. Use the explicit FRONTEND_URL from .env to avoid silent cookie failures across origins.
set_session_cookie uses samesite='none'; secure=True — correct for cross-site preview, but combined with CORS '*' may misbehave; tighten origins.
calc_streak runs an O(60) sequential mongo find per habit per request — add a date range query + group, or cache per request.
/api/coach/chat catches and exposes raw exception string (`str(e)[:120]`) to the client — leaks internal stack frames; replace with generic message and log the rest.

/api/billing/verify accepts any signature when secret starts with 'placeholder' — clearly intentional for dev, but ensure this branch is gated on an explicit `DEV_MODE` flag before going to production.
data-testid coverage is inconsistent — many list-rendered interactive elements (plan buttons, reminder actions, habit tiles, onboarding chips) miss testids.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@lifecue.ai / Admin@2026 — confirmed working. Auto-generated test users used `e2e_<ts>@lifecueqa.com` / `TestPass@2026`.
seed_data_creation: Admin user is auto-seeded on backend startup. Pytest creates and deletes its own TEST_ users via /api/auth/register and /api/privacy/account.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes (1) Coach model ids, (2) missing data-testids on Subscription/Onboarding/Today, (3) duplicate React keys — re-run /app/backend/tests/test_lifecue_backend.py and re-run the Playwright flow (login admin → today water → coach send → subscription monthly → profile save). Backend tests already idempotent. Email validation requires non-reserved TLDs (use lifecueqa.com).
rca of the issue: AI Coach 500: server.py TIER_MODELS uses `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` which the Emergent LLM gateway / litellm rejects with 'Invalid model name'. The error originates inside emergentintegrations.LlmChat → litellm.completion. Reproduce: POST /api/coach/chat {message:'hi', tier:'quick'} as any authenticated user. Mitigation: change `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` to `gemini-2.0-flash` (or similar known-good id), verify daily/deep model ids, and wrap the call to return a friendly 503 instead of leaking the litellm error string.
